subject: [PATCH] mm: mempolicy: fix automatic numa balancing for shmem

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary: Reviewer Gregory Price expressed concern about the patch's impact on users, specifically noting that silently inherited policies (MPOL_F_MOF / MPOL_F_MORON) will be confusing and suggested adding documentation to clarify the default global policy as MPOL_LOCAL + (MPOL_F_MOF | MPOL_F_MORON)
reviews.raw_body:
I lean towards Johannes' stance here that consistency is better, and if
KVM has a known-poor interaction with numa balancing, maybe KVM should
simply set a default policy (when none are present) to avoid this.

That said, this is painful because MPOL_F_MOF / MPOL_F_MORON are already
a silently inherited policy on boot (added during init).  This will be
confusing for users.

This is not documented anywhere.  Maybe this change should come with a
docs addition that says the default global policy is ACTUALLY:

   MPOL_LOCAL + (MPOL_F_MOF | MPOL_F_MORON)

Instead of just MPOL_LOCAL and letting that silent implementation
detail bite people.

~Gregory

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary: Johannes Weiner pointed out that the documentation does not clearly state the default global policy for numa balancing, specifically mentioning that MPOL_F_MOF / MPOL_F_MORON are internal flags and should not be silently inherited on boot. He suggested adding a doc addition to clarify this.
reviews.raw_body:
Hm, where do you see "Instead of just MPOL_LOCAL"?

Documentation/filesystems/tmpfs.rst says it defaults to "default" and
points me to set_mempolicy(2). That in turn in general makes little
mention of numa balancing and doesn't mention MPOL_F_MOF/MPOL_F_MORON.
In fact these are documented as internal flags.

The numa_balancing entry in
Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst however DOES say plainly
that enabling this feature will sample and migrate mapped process
memory to where it's used, and that this has a performance penalty.

It seems there is a general lack of documented interactions between
user-requested policies and the system-wide numa-balancing behavior.

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that MPOL_F_NUMA_BALANCING is an internal flag, essentially a shorthand for (F_MOF | F_MORON), and explained its usage as 'internal' and generated by passing this flag.

Author acknowledged Johannes' feedback about the silent implementation detail of MPOL_F_MOF / MPOL_F_MORON being inherited on boot, and suggested setting MPOL_F_NUMA_BALANCING in default/preferred policies to make it clear that numa balancing is enabled by default. The author proposes documenting this behavior.

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that Johannes' fix is correct and there are no additional changes required to address the issue of KVM interacting poorly with numa balancing, confirming that users enabling numa balancing while running KVM will get the expected performance hit.
reviews.raw_body:
Walking this all back:

  - MPOL_DEFAULT is the default policy
  - numa_balancing=on means enable NUMA_BALANCING on the defaul policy
  - shmem was broken and did not respect this

Johannes' fix is correct, and there are no additional changes required.

Users enabling numa balancing while running KVM with shared regions are
not getting what they're asking for presently - that is a bug.

They should get the performance hit they're asking for (and then either
turn off numa balancing or set an explicit mempolicy for those tasks /
memfd's).

~Gregory

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: Reviewer suggested adding logging statements (pr_warn() or pr_info()) to help users identify the source of regressions caused by silent implementation details in MPOL_F_MOF and MPOL_F_MORON policies.
reviews.raw_body:
Does it make sense to add a pr_warn() or pr_info() to help users
figure this out when they see a regression?

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged the reviewer's concern about the silent implementation detail of MPOL_F_MOF / MPOL_F_MORON being inherited by default, but expressed uncertainty and skepticism about the proposed solution.
reviews.raw_body:
I'm not sure in what scenario we would do this that doesn't simple
reduce to "You turned numa balancing on".

Not against it.

~Gregory

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer Andrew Morton suggested adding a mechanism to detect when numa balancing is disabled for shmem and emit a one-time warning message, in addition to documentation improvements.
reviews.raw_body:
Is there anything we can do to make this process less painful? 
Documentation of course, but what about detecting the situation and
emitting a once-off warning?

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary: reviewer suggested adding a rate-limited or one-time warning message on the first PROT_NONE fault affecting KVM in the painful zap path to help diagnose issues
reviews.raw_body:
Maybe a rate-limited or one-time pr_info/warn on the first PROT_NONE
fault affecting KVM in the painful zap path?

Non-invasive, targeted, and helpful.

~Gregory

2026-07-23.patch_summary: This patch addresses a problem with automatic NUMA balancing for shared memory (shmem) by fixing the default policy used when no explicit policy is set. The issue arises from silently inherited policies on boot, which can be confusing for users and lead to unexpected behavior. To resolve this, the patch proposes setting a default global policy that includes MPOL_LOCAL and additional flags to ensure consistent NUMA balancing.
